name: cortex-vscode
description: Browse, search, and manage Cortex knowledge directly from VS Code.
author: Ala Arab

cortex.mcpServerPath.markdownDescription: Absolute path to the Cortex MCP server entrypoint (`index.js`). Leave blank to auto-detect from a global `npm install -g cortex`.

cortex.nodePath.markdownDescription: Path to the Node.js binary used to spawn the MCP server. VS Code's built-in Electron binary won't work — this must point to a real `node` install.

cortex.storePath.markdownDescription: Path to the Cortex store directory (where projects, findings, and config live). Leave blank to use `~/.cortex`.

cortex.onboardingComplete.description: Internal flag — set to true after first-run onboarding completes. Reset to false to re-trigger onboarding.

enumDescriptions: Minimal — Only save findings when you explicitly ask (e.g. 'remember this'). Zero auto-capture. Best for: users who want full manual control.
Conservative — Save decisions and pitfalls only. Auto-capture up to 3 findings per session. Best for: small projects or when noise is a concern.
Balanced — Save non-obvious patterns, decisions, pitfalls, and bugs worth remembering. Auto-capture up to 10 per session. Best for: most projects.
Aggressive — Save everything worth remembering, err on the side of capturing. Auto-capture up to 20 per session. Best for: complex projects with many contributors.
cortex.findingSensitivity.markdownDescription: **Master control for how aggressively Cortex captures findings.** This sets the session cap (how many findings can be auto-captured per session) and the agent instruction (what kinds of insights to save). Higher levels capture more but may produce noise; lower levels are quieter but may miss useful patterns.

enumDescriptions: High — Always auto-capture findings and tasks without waiting for explicit signals. Cortex actively monitors conversations for insights worth saving.
Medium — Only auto-capture when explicit signals are detected (e.g. words like 'add finding', 'worth remembering', tags like [decision], [pitfall]). Quieter but still responsive.
Low — Never auto-capture. Cortex only saves findings and tasks when you explicitly use a command or MCP tool. Fully manual.
cortex.proactivity.markdownDescription: **Global proactivity level** for both findings and tasks. Controls whether Cortex auto-captures insights or waits for explicit signals. Override per-type with the findings and tasks settings below.

enumDescriptions: High — Always auto-capture findings (patterns, decisions, bugs) from conversations.
Medium — Only capture findings when explicit signals detected (e.g. 'remember this', [decision] tags).
Low — Never auto-capture findings. Only saved via explicit add_finding calls.
cortex.proactivityFindings.markdownDescription: **Override proactivity for findings only.** When set, this takes priority over the global `#cortex.proactivity#` for finding capture. Leave at the same level as global proactivity if you don't need a separate override.

enumDescriptions: High — Always auto-capture tasks from conversations (e.g. 'we should fix X' → task created).
Medium — Only capture tasks when explicit signals detected (e.g. 'add this to tasks', 'put that on the todo list').
Low — Never auto-capture tasks. Only created via explicit add_task calls.
cortex.proactivityTasks.markdownDescription: **Override proactivity for tasks only.** When set, this takes priority over the global `#cortex.proactivity#` for task capture. Leave at the same level as global proactivity if you don't need a separate override.

enumDescriptions: Off — Task system completely disabled. No task tracking, no task injection into context.
Manual — Tasks only created when you explicitly request them (e.g. 'add task: fix the login bug').
Suggest — Agent suggests tasks but waits for your confirmation before adding them. Good for reviewing before committing.
Auto — Agent adds tasks automatically when it detects actionable work items in conversation. Most hands-off.
cortex.taskMode.markdownDescription: **Task automation mode.** Controls how the agent interacts with the task system. Separate from proactivity — this controls the agent's permission to create/manage tasks, while proactivity controls *when* it tries to.

cortex.autoExtract.markdownDescription: **Extract insights from project context on each prompt.** When enabled, the hook-prompt pipeline scans the current project for keywords and injects relevant cortex findings into the agent's context. Disable if you want cortex to be purely manual (search_knowledge only).

cortex.autoCapture.markdownDescription: **Extract insights from conversation transcripts at session end.** When enabled, cortex analyzes the full conversation after a session ends and captures findings it missed during the session. Useful for catching patterns that only become apparent in retrospect. Requires an active LLM endpoint.

cortex.hooksEnabled.markdownDescription: **Master switch for cortex hooks.** When disabled, no hooks fire on prompts, session starts, or stops — cortex becomes a passive MCP server that only responds when explicitly called. Hooks handle: context injection (SessionStart), knowledge retrieval (UserPromptSubmit), and auto-save (Stop).

cortex.semanticDedup.markdownDescription:
**Semantic duplicate detection for offline maintenance.** Used by `cortex maintain consolidate` and `cortex maintain extract` when no agent session is active. Detects findings that say the same thing in different words and merges them.

- **Live sessions**: Dedup uses the active agent (Claude/GPT) at no extra cost.
- **Batch mode**: Uses a separate LLM call per finding group. Results cached 24h.
- **Cost**: ~$0.01/batch-session with Haiku.

cortex.semanticConflict.markdownDescription:
**Semantic conflict detection for offline maintenance.** Used by `cortex maintain consolidate` and `cortex maintain extract` when no agent session is active. Detects findings that contradict each other (e.g. 'always use X' vs 'never use X') and annotates them.

- **Live sessions**: Conflict detection uses the active agent at no extra cost.
- **Batch mode**: Uses a separate LLM call per finding group. Results cached 24h.
- **Cost**: ~$0.01/batch-session with Haiku.

enumDescriptions: Server default (Haiku) — cheapest option, recommended
Claude Haiku 4.5 — fast and cheap, best for batch operations (~$0.01/session)
Claude Sonnet 4.6 — higher quality, ~10x more expensive than Haiku
Claude Opus 4.6 — highest quality but very expensive for batch; not recommended
GPT-4o mini — cost-effective OpenAI alternative for batch operations
GPT-4o — expensive for batch operations; not recommended
cortex.llmModel.markdownDescription:
**LLM model for offline cortex operations** (`consolidate`, `extract`). Only used when semantic dedup or conflict detection is enabled above.

- **Live sessions**: The active agent handles dedup/conflict — this setting is ignored.
- **Batch mode**: This model runs the analysis. Haiku is recommended for cost.
- Sets the `CORTEX_LLM_MODEL` environment variable.

scripts.compile: tsc -p ./ && node -e "try{require('fs').copyFileSync(require('path').resolve(__dirname,'..','mcp','dist','memory-ui-graph.js'),require('path').resolve(__dirname,'out','memory-ui-graph.js'));console.log('Copied memory-ui-graph.js to out/')}catch(e){console.warn('Note: mcp/dist/memory-ui-graph.js not found, graph engine will use repo-relative path')}"
